summary: Recent updates to the libwebrtc build process introduced two new Python scripts aimed at improving how developers manage their code. One script checks for any missing branch-head commits, helping ensure that all crucial updates and changes are tracked effectively, while the other provides a visual overview of those branch-head commits. These enhancements are significant because they support better collaboration and maintenance of WebRTC, the technology behind video calls, making it easier for engineers to keep the codebase up to date and reduce the risk of issues arising from overlooked changes.
